seoTitle: How to Use a Lookup Field in Airtable
metaDescription: Add a Lookup field in Airtable to pull data from linked records, apply conditional filters, and format the fetched values correctly.
intro: A Lookup field pulls a specific piece of data from a linked record in another table, so you don't have to duplicate information manually across tables. This guide covers setting up the initial link, adding the Lookup field, and filtering or formatting what it displays.

steps.heading: Create a Link to Another Record field
steps.body: Before adding a lookup, set up a 'Link to another record' field to establish the base connection between your two tables.

steps.heading: Add a Lookup field
steps.body: Click to add a new field and select the specialized Lookup data type.

steps.heading: Choose the linked field and target column
steps.body: Select your target linked record field and choose the specific column you want to fetch from the connected table.

steps.heading: Apply conditional filters
steps.body: Optionally apply conditional parameters to filter out irrelevant information from the lookup results.

steps.heading: Format the output
steps.body: Adjust formatting options so the fetched values display correctly, whether they're text, dates, or currency.

steps.heading: Verify the sync
steps.body: Review the live fetched data to confirm the relational connection is pulling the correct values.

faq.question: Do I need a Link field before I can add a Lookup field?
faq.answer: Yes, a Lookup field depends on an existing Link to Another Record field to know which linked table to pull data from.

faq.question: Can a Lookup field pull more than one value?
faq.answer: Yes, if a record is linked to multiple records, the Lookup field will display multiple values from the connected column.

faq.question: What's the difference between a Lookup and a Rollup field?
faq.answer: A Lookup field simply displays linked data as-is, while a Rollup field lets you aggregate it with a function like sum or count.

faq.question: Can I filter what a Lookup field pulls in?
faq.answer: Yes, you can apply conditional filtering rules to limit which linked records' data actually shows up in the lookup output.
